title: Goeppertia macilenta
taxonomy: Goeppertia macilenta is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The classification of Goeppertia macilenta can be broken down as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Monocots, Order - Zingiberales, Family - Marantaceae. The genus Goeppertia is often confused with Calathea due to similarities in their leaf patterns. However, Goeppertia is distinct in its flower and growth characteristics.
uses: Goeppertia macilenta is a species of flowering plant that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its uses. In traditional medicine, Goeppertia macilenta has been used to treat digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems.
